name: Relativity
description: Special relativity (1905) and general relativity (1915) — Einstein's theories of space, time, and gravity. Special relativity unifies space and time into spacetime and reveals the equivalence of mass and energy. General relativity extends this to describe gravity as the curvature of spacetime produced by mass-energy.
axioms: Special relativity: the laws of physics are the same in all inertial reference frames
Light postulate: the speed of light in vacuum, c, is constant in all inertial frames
Spacetime interval: ds² = -c²dt² + dx² + dy² + dz² is invariant under Lorentz transformations
Equivalence principle: gravitational and inertial mass are identical; locally, gravity is indistinguishable from acceleration
General relativity: mass-energy tells spacetime how to curve; curved spacetime tells mass-energy how to move (Einstein field equations)
Geodesic equation: free-falling particles follow straightest possible paths (geodesics) in curved spacetime
The cosmological constant Λ can be included in the Einstein field equations: G_μν + Λg_μν = (8πG/c⁴)T_μν
reasoning_patterns: Tensor calculus: systematic index manipulation with covariant/contravariant components and Einstein summation
Metric analysis: from a given metric, extract distances, angles, time dilation, and causal structure
Geodesic computation: solve the geodesic equation to find particle trajectories and light paths in curved spacetime
Symmetry exploitation: identify Killing vectors → conserved quantities along geodesics in GR
Limiting case reduction: reduce to Newtonian gravity when v ≪ c and the gravitational field is weak
Lorentz transformation: calculate time dilation, length contraction, and relativity of simultaneity in SR
Stress-energy analysis: construct T_μν for the matter/energy distribution to source the Einstein equations
boundary_conditions: Singularities (black hole centers, Big Bang) signal the breakdown of classical GR — needs quantum gravity
Does not cover quantum gravity — GR and quantum mechanics are mathematically incompatible (non-renormalizable)
Dark energy requires a cosmological constant or modified gravity — the nature of dark energy is unknown
Gravitational wave detection is observational, not theoretical — GR predicted them in 1916, LIGO confirmed in 2015
No preferred frame exists — Mach's principle (that inertia derives from distant masses) is not fully incorporated
key_references: Einstein, A. (1905). Zur Elektrodynamik bewegter Körper (SR)
Einstein, A. (1915). Die Feldgleichungen der Gravitation (GR)
Penrose, R. The Road to Reality (GR chapters)
canonical_truths: E = mc² — mass and energy are equivalent; the most famous equation in physics
Time dilation: moving clocks run slower (confirmed by GPS satellites to ~10⁻⁹ precision)
Gravitational lensing: light bends around massive objects (first confirmed by Eddington 1919 eclipse expedition)
Black holes exist — confirmed by LIGO gravitational wave detections (GW150914) and Event Horizon Telescope imaging (M87*)
The universe is expanding (Hubble 1929) and accelerating (Riess, Perlmutter, Schmidt 1998 — Nobel 2011)
Gravitational waves travel at c — confirmed by GW170817 electromagnetic counterpart detection
